Package neqsim.process.diagnostics

Class EvidenceCollector

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.diagnostics.EvidenceCollector
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class EvidenceCollector extends Object implements Serializable
Analyzes historian and STID data to collect evidence for or against each hypothesis.

The collector compares observed historian behavior against the expected diagnostic signals stored on each hypothesis. Evidence is only attached to a hypothesis when a tag matches one of its fingerprints, unless the hypothesis has no fingerprints, in which case legacy generic evidence is returned for backward compatibility.

Version:
1.1
Author:
NeqSim Development Team
See Also:

  • Method Details

    • setHistorianData

      public void setHistorianData(Map<String,double[]> data, double[] timestamps)
      Sets the historian time-series data.
      Parameters:
      data - map of parameter name to time-series values
      timestamps - array of timestamps (Unix epoch seconds) parallel to data arrays

    • setStidData

      public void setStidData(Map<String,String> stidData)
      Sets STID metadata.
      Parameters:
      stidData - map of parameter to STID value/description

    • setDesignLimit

      public void setDesignLimit(String parameter, double lowLimit, double highLimit)
      Sets a design limit for a parameter.
      Parameters:
      parameter - parameter name
      lowLimit - low limit, or Double.NaN if no low limit applies
      highLimit - high limit, or Double.NaN if no high limit applies

    • loadFromCsv

      public void loadFromCsv(String csvPath) throws IOException
      Loads historian data from a CSV file.

      Expected format: first column is timestamp, remaining columns are parameter values. First row is a header with parameter names.

      Parameters:
      csvPath - path to CSV file
      Throws:
      IOException - if file cannot be read

    • collectEvidence

      public List<Hypothesis.Evidence> collectEvidence(Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Collects hypothesis-specific evidence by analyzing all available data.
      Parameters:
      hypothesis - the hypothesis to evaluate
      Returns:
      list of evidence items found

    • calculateLikelihoodScore

      public double calculateLikelihoodScore(List<Hypothesis.Evidence> evidenceList)
      Calculates the aggregate likelihood score from evidence items.
      Parameters:
      evidenceList - evidence items
      Returns:
      score in range 0 to 1

    • addIfNotNull

      private void addIfNotNull(List<Hypothesis.Evidence> evidenceList, Hypothesis.Evidence evidence)
      Adds an evidence item when it is not null.
      Parameters:
      evidenceList - target evidence list
      evidence - evidence item, possibly null

    • strengthWeight

      private double strengthWeight(Hypothesis.EvidenceStrength strength)
      Returns the numerical weight for an evidence strength.
      Parameters:
      strength - evidence strength
      Returns:
      numerical weight

    • evidenceContribution

      private double evidenceContribution(Hypothesis.Evidence evidence)
      Returns the likelihood contribution for one evidence item.
      Parameters:
      evidence - evidence item
      Returns:
      contribution in range 0 to 1

    • analyzeTrend

      private Hypothesis.Evidence analyzeTrend(String param, double[] values, Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Analyzes trend in a time-series.
      Parameters:
      param - parameter name
      values - data values
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      evidence item or null if no significant trend matches the hypothesis

    • trendStrength

      private Hypothesis.EvidenceStrength trendStrength(double rSquared, double changePercent)
      Determines trend evidence strength from fit quality and magnitude.
      Parameters:
      rSquared - regression coefficient of determination
      changePercent - approximate percent change over the window
      Returns:
      evidence strength

    • regressionX

      private double regressionX(int index)
      Returns the x-coordinate used in trend regression.
      Parameters:
      index - sample index
      Returns:
      timestamp if available, otherwise the sample index

    • analyzeThreshold

      private Hypothesis.Evidence analyzeThreshold(String param, double[] values, Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Analyzes design-limit exceedance.
      Parameters:
      param - parameter name
      values - data values
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      evidence item or null if limits are not exceeded or not relevant

    • findLimitByAlias

      private double[] findLimitByAlias(String param)
      Finds a design limit whose key aliases the parameter name.
      Parameters:
      param - parameter name
      Returns:
      low/high limit pair, or null if not found

    • analyzeRateOfChange

      private Hypothesis.Evidence analyzeRateOfChange(String param, double[] values, Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Analyzes rate of change to detect step changes.
      Parameters:
      param - parameter name
      values - data values
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      evidence item or null if no significant step change matches the hypothesis

    • analyzeChangePoint

      private Hypothesis.Evidence analyzeChangePoint(String param, double[] values, Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Detects change points by comparing the mean of the first and second halves of the data.

      A change point is flagged when the difference between half-means exceeds 2 standard deviations of the full series, indicating a statistically significant shift in operating regime.

      Parameters:
      param - parameter name
      values - data values
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      evidence item or null if no significant change point found

    • analyzeAcceleration

      private Hypothesis.Evidence analyzeAcceleration(String param, double[] values, Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Detects acceleration (trend getting worse faster) by comparing slopes of the first and second halves of the data.

      If the absolute slope doubles between the first and second halves, it indicates an accelerating degradation which is a strong indicator of imminent failure.

      Parameters:
      param - parameter name
      values - data values
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      evidence item or null if no significant acceleration found

    • computeSlope

      private double computeSlope(double[] values, int from, int to)
      Computes the linear regression slope over a sub-range of the data.
      Parameters:
      values - data values
      from - start index (inclusive)
      to - end index (exclusive)
      Returns:
      slope, or 0 if insufficient data

    • analyzeCorrelations

      private List<Hypothesis.Evidence> analyzeCorrelations(Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Analyzes correlations between parameters.
      Parameters:
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      list of correlation evidence

    • analyzeMultiParameterPattern

      private List<Hypothesis.Evidence> analyzeMultiParameterPattern(Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Analyzes multi-parameter patterns specific to the hypothesis's expected signals.

      When a hypothesis expects multiple parameters to move in specific directions simultaneously (e.g., vibration increasing AND efficiency decreasing), this method checks whether the historian data confirms the combined fingerprint. A hypothesis whose full signal set is present gets a STRONG "pattern match" evidence item.

      Parameters:
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      list of multi-parameter evidence items

    • classifyBehavior

      private Hypothesis.ExpectedBehavior classifyBehavior(double[] values)
      Classifies the dominant behavior of a time series.
      Parameters:
      values - data values
      Returns:
      INCREASE, DECREASE, or null if no clear trend

    • crossReferenceStid

      private List<Hypothesis.Evidence> crossReferenceStid(Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Cross-references STID design conditions.
      Parameters:
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      list of STID-based evidence

    • findHistorianValues

      private double[] findHistorianValues(String parameter)
      Finds historian values by exact key or alias match.
      Parameters:
      parameter - parameter alias
      Returns:
      values or null if not found

    • latestValid

      private double latestValid(double[] values)
      Returns the latest valid value from an array.
      Parameters:
      values - time-series values
      Returns:
      latest non-NaN value, or NaN if none exists

    • createEvidenceForObservedBehavior

      private Hypothesis.Evidence createEvidenceForObservedBehavior(String param, String observation, Hypothesis.EvidenceStrength strength, String source, Hypothesis.ExpectedBehavior observedBehavior, Hypothesis hypothesis)
      Converts an observed behavior into evidence for the supplied hypothesis.
      Parameters:
      param - parameter name
      observation - observed behavior text
      strength - base evidence strength
      source - source identifier
      observedBehavior - observed behavior classification
      hypothesis - hypothesis being evaluated
      Returns:
      evidence item, or null if the observation is irrelevant to the hypothesis

    • findMatchingSignal

      private Hypothesis.ExpectedSignal findMatchingSignal(Hypothesis hypothesis, String param, Hypothesis.ExpectedBehavior observedBehavior)
      Finds the best expected signal for a parameter and observed behavior.
      Parameters:
      hypothesis - hypothesis containing expected signals
      param - observed parameter name
      observedBehavior - observed behavior
      Returns:
      matching signal, or null if none matches the parameter

    • behaviorMatches

      private boolean behaviorMatches(Hypothesis.ExpectedBehavior expected, Hypothesis.ExpectedBehavior observed)
      Checks if the observed behavior supports the expected behavior.
      Parameters:
      expected - expected behavior
      observed - observed behavior
      Returns:
      true if observed behavior supports the expected behavior

    • isContradictory

      private boolean isContradictory(Hypothesis.ExpectedBehavior expected, Hypothesis.ExpectedBehavior observed)
      Checks whether the observed behavior contradicts the expected behavior.
      Parameters:
      expected - expected behavior
      observed - observed behavior
      Returns:
      true if the observed behavior is a meaningful contradiction

    • matchesPattern

      private boolean matchesPattern(String parameter, String pattern)
      Checks if a parameter name matches a pipe-separated alias pattern.
      Parameters:
      parameter - parameter name
      pattern - alias pattern
      Returns:
      true when any alias matches

    • normalize

      private String normalize(String text)
      Normalizes a parameter name for alias matching.
      Parameters:
      text - input text
      Returns:
      lower-case alphanumeric text

    • pearsonCorrelation

      private double pearsonCorrelation(double[] x, double[] y)
      Calculates Pearson correlation coefficient between two arrays.
      Parameters:
      x - first array
      y - second array
      Returns:
      correlation coefficient in range -1 to 1, or 0 if insufficient data
